section.heading: Industrial mobilization: Board on Mobilization of Industries Essential for Military Preparedness

content.p: The President may appoint a nonpartisan Board on Mobilization of Industries Essential for Military Preparedness, and may provide necessary clerical assistance, to organize and coordinate operations under sections 4882 and 4883 of this title.

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 103–160, div. A, title VIII, § 822(a)(1), Nov. 30, 1993, 107 Stat. 1705, § 2540; renumbered § 2539a, Pub. L. 103–337, div. A, title X, § 1070(a)(13)(A), Oct. 5, 1994, 108 Stat. 2856; renumbered § 4884 and amended Pub. L. 116–283, div. A, title XVIII, § 1870(e)(2), Jan. 1, 2021, 134 Stat. 4286.)

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: Provisions similar to those in this section were contained in sections 4502(d) and 9502(d) of this title, prior to repeal by Pub. L. 103–160, § 822(a)(2).

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: Pub. L. 116–283, § 1870(e)(2)(A), renumbered section 2539a of this title as this section.
1994—Pub. L. 103–337 renumbered section 2540 of this title as section 2539a.

note.heading: Effective Date of 2021 Amendment
note.p: Amendment by Pub. L. 116–283 effective Jan. 1, 2022, with additional provisions for delayed implementation and applicability of existing law, see section 1801(d) of Pub. L. 116–283, set out as a note preceding section 3001 of this title.
